titlestmt.titleproper: J. J. Phillips correspondence with Peter B. Howard regarding publication of Nigga in the woodpile
Phillips (J. J.) correspondence with Peter B. Howard regarding publication of Nigga in the woodpile
titlestmt.author: by Gabriela Redwine

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 23:53

did.unittitle: J. J. Phillips correspondence with Peter B. Howard regarding publication of Nigga in the Woodpile

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: This collection consists of a flash drive containing two computer files with J. J. Phillips' email correspondence with Peter B. Howard from July through November 2008.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Two computer files containing email correspondence with Peter B. Howard from July to November 2008. The majority of the emails are from Phillips to Howard. In the course of their correspondence about the publication of her poem Nigga in the Woodpile, Phillips provides context for Mojo Hand (1966, 1987), describes other people's assumptions about her as a Black author following the novel's publication, and shares a variety of personal memories and experiences.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. Box 1 (Flash drive): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: J. J. Phillips Correspondence with Peter B. Howard Regarding Publication of Nigga in the Woodpile is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscsript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: J. J. Phillips Correspondence with Peter B. Howard Regarding Publication of Nigga in the Woodpile. James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.physdesc: 4 Gigabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies may be requested. Contact Access Services for further information.
